mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0% for three consecutive months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broadening consumer demand and construction activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals improving manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 50% of months where INDPRO rises, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of elevated market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with subdued inflation expectations reduce real discount rates and preserve corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding employment and rising aggregate wages, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where PAYEMS increases, or the signal shows zero predictive IC over a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below -0.5% quarter-over-quarter, severe demand contraction forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 declines by more than 0.5%, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand recovery, which triggers short-term equity momentum and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of aligned months, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.
